name.reasoning: Direct transliteration of Hindi name अर्जुन शर्मा to English.

age.reasoning: वर्ष means 'years' in Hindi. Age is 58 years.

sex.reasoning: पुरुष is the Hindi word for 'Male'.

diagnoses.reasoning: टाइप 2 मधुमेह मेलिटस is a direct Hindi transliteration of 'Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus'. Duration of 12 years noted. Maps clearly to ICD-10 E11.9 and OMOP 201826.

diagnoses.original_text: कोरोनरी आर्टरी डिजीज (2021 में CABG किया गया)
diagnoses.reasoning: कोरोनरी आर्टरी डिजीज is a Hindi transliteration of 'Coronary Artery Disease'. History of CABG in 2021 confirms established CAD. Maps to ICD-10 I25.10 and OMOP 317576.
BP of 148/88 mmHg exceeds 140/90 threshold for hypertension. Patient is also on Ramipril (an antihypertensive), supporting this diagnosis. Not explicitly listed as a diagnosis but clinically evident.

diagnoses.original_text: LDL: 2.8 mmol/L; HDL: 0.9 mmol/L; ट्राइग्लिसराइड: 3.2 mmol/L
diagnoses.reasoning: Elevated LDL (2.8 > 2.6), low HDL (0.9 < 1.0 for males), and markedly elevated triglycerides (3.2 >> 1.7) together constitute mixed dyslipidemia. Patient is on Atorvastatin. Inferred diagnosis from lab values.

eGFR of 58 mL/min/1.73m² falls within Stage 3a (45-59) range. Elevated creatinine at 112 µmol/L supports renal impairment. Clinician note mentions monitoring eGFR. CKD Stage 3 (borderline 3a/3b) mapped to 3b given the lower end of 3a range; however, a single reading may need confirmation.

BMI of 29.8 is just below the WHO obesity threshold of 30, technically classifying as overweight. However, for South Asian populations, lower BMI thresholds for obesity are often applied (≥27.5). Mapped as borderline obesity per South Asian criteria but flagged for review.

medications.original_text: मेटफॉर्मिन 1000 मिग्रा — दिन में दो बार (सुबह-शाम, भोजन के साथ)
medications.standardized_english_term: Metformin 1000 mg twice daily (morning and evening, with meals)
medications.reasoning: मेटफॉर्मिन is a direct Hindi transliteration of Metformin. Dose 1000 mg, frequency BID with meals is clearly stated.

medications.original_text: एटोरवास्टेटिन 40 मिग्रा — रात को सोते समय
medications.reasoning: एटोरवास्टेटिन is a Hindi transliteration of Atorvastatin. Dose 40 mg, taken at bedtime (रात को सोते समय).

medications.standardized_english_term: Aspirin 75 mg once daily in the morning
medications.reasoning: एस्पिरिन is a Hindi transliteration of Aspirin. Dose 75 mg daily in the morning. Low-dose aspirin consistent with CAD secondary prevention.
रामिप्रिल is a Hindi transliteration of Ramipril. Dose 5 mg taken at night. ACE inhibitor for cardio-renal protection.
ग्लिमेपिराइड is a Hindi transliteration of Glimepiride. Dose 2 mg before breakfast. Sulfonylurea for glycemic control.

vitals.reasoning: रक्तचाप means 'blood pressure' in Hindi. Systolic component is 148 mmHg, above the 140 mmHg threshold for hypertension.
Diastolic component of रक्तचाप (blood pressure) is 88 mmHg, approaching the 90 mmHg hypertension threshold.
हृदय गति means 'heart rate' in Hindi. 72 bpm is within normal range (60-100).
वजन means 'weight' and किग्रा means 'kg' in Hindi. Body weight is 84 kg.
BMI of 29.8 kg/m² calculated from weight 84 kg and height 168 cm. Overweight by WHO standard; obese by South Asian criteria (≥27.5).

lab_values.reasoning: उपवास रक्त शर्करा translates to 'fasting blood glucose' in Hindi. Value 9.4 mmol/L is significantly above the reference range of 3.9-5.5 mmol/L, indicating poor glycemic control.
HbA1c of 8.6% is above the target of <7.0%, indicating suboptimal diabetes control. Clinician also notes HbA1c is above target and recommends adding SGLT2 inhibitor.
कुल कोलेस्ट्रॉल means 'total cholesterol' in Hindi. Value 5.1 mmol/L is just below the upper limit of 5.2 mmol/L but still elevated for a CAD patient where stricter targets apply.
LDL cholesterol at 2.8 mmol/L exceeds the reference of <2.6 mmol/L. For a post-CABG CAD patient, target LDL should ideally be <1.8 mmol/L, making this significantly above goal.
HDL at 0.9 mmol/L is below the reference of >1.0 mmol/L for males, indicating low protective cholesterol.
ट्राइग्लिसराइड is Hindi transliteration of 'triglyceride'. Value 3.2 mmol/L is nearly double the upper reference limit of 1.7 mmol/L, indicating significant hypertriglyceridemia.
क्रिएटिनिन is Hindi transliteration of 'creatinine'. Value 112 µmol/L exceeds the upper reference of 106 µmol/L, suggesting renal impairment.
eGFR of 58 mL/min/1.73m² is below the reference of >60, indicating CKD Stage 3a (45-59 range). Clinician recommends monitoring.

variants.original_text: rsID=rs7903146  GENE=TCF7L2  GT=1/1  CLNSIG=risk_factor  AF_SAS=0.28  PHENOTYPE=Type_2_Diabetes
variants.standardized_english_term: TCF7L2 rs7903146 — Homozygous risk allele for Type 2 Diabetes
variants.reasoning: TCF7L2 rs7903146 is one of the strongest genetic risk factors for T2D. Homozygous genotype (1/1) confers highest risk. Well-established in South Asian populations with AF of 0.28. Phenotype maps directly to T2D (OMOP 201826).

variants.original_text: rsID=rs1801282  GENE=PPARG  GT=0/1  CLNSIG=protective  AF_SAS=0.04  PHENOTYPE=Type_2_Diabetes_T2D
variants.standardized_english_term: PPARG rs1801282 (Pro12Ala) — Heterozygous protective variant for Type 2 Diabetes
variants.reasoning: PPARG Pro12Ala (rs1801282) is a well-known protective variant for T2D. Heterozygous carrier status provides partial protection. Low frequency in South Asian population (0.04). Phenotype maps to T2D (OMOP 201826).

variants.original_text: rsID=rs2383206  GENE=CDKN2A_CDKN2B  GT=1/1  CLNSIG=risk_factor  AF_SAS=0.52  PHENOTYPE=Coronary_Artery_Disease
variants.standardized_english_term: CDKN2A/CDKN2B rs2383206 — Homozygous risk allele for Coronary Artery Disease
variants.reasoning: 9p21 locus (CDKN2A/CDKN2B) rs2383206 is a well-replicated CAD risk variant. Homozygous genotype with high AF in South Asians (0.52). Consistent with patient's established CAD and CABG history. Maps to CAD (OMOP 317576).

variants.original_text: rsID=rs11206510  GENE=PCSK9  GT=0/1  CLNSIG=risk_factor  AF_SAS=0.18  PHENOTYPE=LDL_Cholesterol_CAD
variants.standardized_english_term: PCSK9 rs11206510 — Heterozygous risk variant for elevated LDL Cholesterol and CAD
variants.reasoning: PCSK9 rs11206510 is associated with LDL cholesterol levels and CAD risk. Heterozygous carrier. The combined phenotype of elevated LDL and CAD is mapped to CAD (OMOP 317576). Patient's LDL is indeed above target.

flags: HbA1c 8.6% significantly above target (<7.0%) — clinician recommends adding SGLT2 inhibitor (e.g., Empagliflozin)
eGFR 58 mL/min/1.73m² below normal — CKD Stage 3a; requires serial monitoring as noted by clinician
Serum creatinine 112 µmol/L above reference range (62-106) — correlates with reduced eGFR
LDL 2.8 mmol/L above general target; for post-CABG CAD patient, target should be <1.8 mmol/L — consider intensifying statin or adding ezetimibe
Triglycerides 3.2 mmol/L nearly double the upper limit (1.7) — significant hypertriglyceridemia
HDL 0.9 mmol/L below reference (>1.0 for males) — low cardioprotective cholesterol
Blood pressure 148/88 mmHg above target despite Ramipril — may need antihypertensive optimization
BMI 29.8 — overweight by WHO criteria, obese by South Asian-specific criteria (≥27.5 kg/m²)
Homozygous TCF7L2 rs7903146 risk genotype combined with homozygous 9p21 (CDKN2A/CDKN2B) risk genotype — high genetic risk burden for both T2D and CAD
Type 2 Diabetes with declining renal function — consider ICD-10 E11.65 (T2D with diabetic CKD) at next review if CKD confirmed on repeat testing
